[[JA:葛木男神社]]

{{wikidata link|Q18991490}}

== Japanese content ==
Katsurakio Shrine

Katsurakio Shrine is a Shinto shrine in Nunoshida, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture, Japan. It is an Engishiki-listed shrine and formerly held village-level status before later reclassification.

Current enshrined deities
- Takamimusubi no kami
- Katsurakio Okami
- Katsurakihime Okami

Historical note
- In 1972, the related Engishiki shrine Katsurakihime Shrine was merged into Katsurakio Shrine.

Scholarly background
The shrine's deity tradition is debated:
- One line identifies Katsurakio with Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko.
- Another line emphasizes Takamimusubi.
- Local historical interpretation also links the shrine area to very old Tosa district traditions and former local ruling lineages.

The precinct preserves memories of earlier relocation traditions and former paired worship with Katsurakihime.

[[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]]

Katsurakio Shrine

Katsurakio Shrine is a Shinto shrine in Nunoshida, Kochi City, Kochi Prefecture, Japan. It is an Engishiki-listed shrine and formerly held village-level status before later reclassification.

Current enshrined deities
- Takamimusubi no kami
- Katsurakio Okami
- Katsurakihime Okami

Historical note
- In 1972, the related Engishiki shrine Katsurakihime Shrine was merged into Katsurakio Shrine.

Scholarly background
The shrine's deity tradition is debated:
- One line identifies Katsurakio with Katsuragi no Sotsuhiko.
- Another line emphasizes Takamimusubi.
- Local historical interpretation also links the shrine area to very old Tosa district traditions and former local ruling lineages.

The precinct preserves memories of earlier relocation traditions and former paired worship with Katsurakihime.

[[Category:Weird possibly ai botched translations]]

link|Q18991490}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 葛木男神社 |画像 = [[File:Katsurakio-jinja haiden.JPG|250px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|高知県|Q134093}}{{jalink|高知市|Q200251}}{{jalink|布師田|Q38276526}}1358 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|17.61|N|133|36|5.73|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=葛木男神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊神}}
葛木男大神
葛木咩大神 |神体 = 神鏡 |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）2社
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = 高結大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|11月17日|Q3028}}{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}（{{jalink|11月5日|Q2993}}{{Sfn|葛木男神社（角川）|1986年}}） |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=葛木男神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Katsurakio-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] [[File:Katsurakio-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|本殿}}]] '''葛木男神社'''（かつらきおじんじゃ{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}/かずらきのおじんじゃ{{Sfn|葛木男神社（国史）}}）は、{{jalink|高知県|Q134093}}{{jalink|高知市|Q200251}}{{jalink|布師田|Q38276526}}（ぬのしだ）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 {{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}47年（{{jalink|1972年|Q2476}}）に同じく式内社の'''葛木咩神社'''（かつらきひめじんじゃ、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}）を合祀している{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 === 祭神 === 現在の祭神は次の3柱境内説明板。。{{jalink|神体|Q327532}}は鏡とされる{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 * '''{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊神}}'''（たかみむすびのかみ） * '''葛木男大神'''（かつらきおのおおかみ） *: {{jalink|葛城襲津彦|Q11620922|葛城襲津彦命}}{{Sfn|葛木男神社（国史）}}。この葛城襲津彦は、大和葛城地方（現・{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|御所市|Q847334}}一帯）の豪族の{{jalink|葛城氏|Q11620908}}祖とされる{{Sfn|葛木男神社（国史）}}。 * '''葛木咩大神'''（かつらきひめのおおかみ） *: 葛城襲津彦妃命{{Sfn|葛木男神社（国史）}}。合祀した葛木咩神社の祭神になる{{Sfn|葛木男神社（国史）}}。 === 祭神について === {{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}以降の考証史料では、葛木男神社の祭神を{{jalink|葛城襲津彦|Q11620922}}とする説や、{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊命}}とする説が見られる{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}。現在では、その両説を採った祭神としている。 * 葛城襲津彦説 *: 『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』『新撰姓氏録』和泉国皇別 布師臣条。において布師臣（布師氏）を葛城襲津彦の後裔とすることに基づく説。この布師氏が当地に居住し、祖神として葛城襲津彦を祀ったので「布師田」の地名が起こったとする{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 * 高皇産霊命説 *: 『新撰姓氏録』『新撰姓氏録』河内国神別 天神 葛木直条。『新撰姓氏録』大和国神別 天神 葛木忌寸条。において葛木直・葛木忌寸を高魂命（高御魂命/高皇産霊命）の後裔とすることに基づく説。近世に見える社名「高結大明神宮」「高魂大明神宮」「高結社」等はこの祭神に起こるとし、また葛木氏（{{jalink|葛城氏|Q11620908}}）を奉斎氏族と推測する{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}。ただし、同じく高皇産霊命後裔の{{jalink|忌部氏|Q8189229}}を奉斎氏族とする説もある{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}。なお高皇産霊命は性の無い独神であるため、『式内社調査報告』では葛城襲津彦説を有力視する{{Sfn|葛木咩神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 上記とは別に『土佐国風土記』{{jalink|逸文|Q1585442}}『雅事問答』所引『土佐国風土記』逸文。では、 {{Quotation|土左の郡家の内に社あり。神の名（みな）は'''天の河の命'''とせり。その南なる道を下れば社あり。神の名は'''浄川媛の命'''、天の河の神の女（むすめ）なり。その天の河の神は土左の大神の子とせり。|『雅事問答』所引『土佐国風土記』逸文（原文漢文）書き下し文は『新編日本古典文学全集 5 風土記』小学館、2003年（ジャパンナレッジ版）、p. 514による。}} として、{{jalink|土佐郡|Q1132134}}の{{jalink|郡衙|Q11643230|郡家}}内には土左大神（{{jalink|土佐神社|Q704890}}祭神）の{{jalink|御子神|Q352176}}の'''天河命'''（あまのかわのみこと）を祀る社が、郡家南の道には天河命の娘神の'''浄川媛命'''（きよかわひめのみこと）を祀る社があると見える。この天河命・浄川媛命は他文献に記載のない神々であるが、後述のように土佐郡家が葛木男神社の旧鎮座地付近に推測されることから、両神を葛木男神・葛木咩神に比定する説がある『高知県の歴史』山川出版社、2001年、pp. 47-48。『{{jalink|日本古典文学全集|Q11507506|新編日本古典文学全集}} 5 風土記』小学館、2003年（{{jalink|ジャパンナレッジ|Q11309996}}版）、pp. 513-514。。土佐郡家は、古くから存在した天河命の社を取り込む形で成立したと見られており、この一帯が土佐郡成立以前の豪族の{{jalink|都佐国造|Q21653737}}（土佐国造）の中心地であったと推測される。この都佐国造は大和葛城地方の{{jalink|三輪氏}}の流れを汲む{{jalink|賀茂朝臣氏|Q122979457}}に連なる系譜といわれ、当社の社名「葛木」とも関連を有している。その場合、本来の祭神は葛城の高鴨に鎮座する都佐国造の祖・'''{{jalink|味耜高彦根命}}'''であった可能性が非常に高い {{jalink|宝賀寿男|Q11452691}}「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/arisimata/arisima3.htm 土佐中央部の古社と奉斎者]」『古樹紀之房間』、1998年。。しかし古くは葛木男神が土左大神の御子神であったとしても、現在の土佐神社と葛木男神社との間に祭祀関係は伝わっていない。 === 歴史 === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|葛木咩神社旧地}}]] 葛木男神社・葛木咩神社とも創建は不詳{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|葛木咩神社（式内社）|1987年}}。伝承では、元は両社とも現社地の南東方（現・字下附の集落南方）において同じ境内に鎮座したが、葛木咩神社は東南方に移り（{{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|name=葛木咩神社旧地}}）、葛木男神社も{{jalink|国分川|Q11420533}}の増水を避け北西方の現在地に移ったという{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|葛木咩神社（式内社）|1987年}}。なお『土佐国風土記』逸文『釈日本紀』巻12（述義8）一言主神条または巻15（述義11）土左大神以神刀一口進于天皇条所引『土佐国風土記』逸文。では、土左高賀茂大社（{{jalink|土佐神社|Q704890}}）の東4里（約2キロメートル）に土佐郡家があったというが、その地は旧鎮座地の字下附付近と推測される{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|土佐国|Q857330}}{{jalink|土佐郡|Q1132134}}において「葛木男神社」「葛木咩神社」と対を成して記載され、葛木男神社・葛木咩神社とも{{jalink|式内社}}に列している{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|葛木咩神社（式内社）|1987年}}。読みはそれぞれ「カツラキヲノ」「カツラキヒメノ」と振られる{{Sfn|葛木男神社（平凡社）|1983年}}。また『{{jalink|和名抄}}』に見える地名のうちでは、現鎮座地は土佐郡土佐郷の東部付近と推測される{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 その後の変遷は不詳で、『長宗我部地検帳』に記載がないため荒廃したと見られている{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|葛木男神社（角川）|1986年}}。『南路志』によると、{{jalink|近世|Q5308718}}の葛木男神社には社地18代・田地1反22代があって西山寺（南方に所在）が{{jalink|別当寺|Q11396755}}を務め、葛木咩神社には社地34代・田地10代があったという{{Sfn|葛木男神社（角川）|1986年}}。 近世には葛木男神社は「高結大明神社」と称していたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}元年（{{jalink|1868年|Q7717}}）に現社名に改称した{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}。明治5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において葛木男神社は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に、葛木咩神社は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}{{Sfn|葛木咩神社（式内社）|1987年}}。{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}47年（{{jalink|1972年|Q2476}}）には葛木咩神社が葛木男神社に合祀され、現在に至っている{{Sfn|葛木男神社（式内社）|1987年}}。 In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}.In {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{jalink|Takami Musubi||Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{jalink|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{jalink|Katsuragi clan|Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{jalink|Early modern times|Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{jalink|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922}}, and that it is {{jalink|Takami Musubi||Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{jalink|Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{jalink|Katsuragi clan|Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{jalink|Isbun|Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{jalink|Tosa Shrine|Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{jalink|gunga|Q11643230|gunge}} of {{jalink|Tosa District|Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{jalink|Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{jalink|Japan Knowledge|Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{jalink|Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{jalink|Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{jalink|Miwa clan}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{jalink|Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{jalink|Hisao Hoga|Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{jalink|Kokubun River|Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{jalink|Tosa Shrine|Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}}5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} is {{jalink|Tosa Province|Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{jalink|Japanese name extract}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), in {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}.In {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{jalink|Takami Musubi||Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{jalink|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{jalink|Katsuragi clan|Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{jalink|Early modern times|Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{jalink|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922}}, and that it is {{jalink|Takami Musubi||Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{jalink|Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{jalink|Katsuragi clan|Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{jalink|Isbun|Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{jalink|Tosa Shrine|Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{jalink|gunga|Q11643230|gunge}} of {{jalink|Tosa District|Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{jalink|Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{jalink|Japan Knowledge|Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{jalink|Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{jalink|Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{jalink|Miwa clan}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{jalink|Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{jalink|Hisao Hoga|Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{jalink|Kokubun River|Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{jalink|Tosa Shrine|Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}}5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} is {{jalink|Tosa Province|Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{jalink|Japanese name extract}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), in {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Katsuragi Ookami
Katsuragimaki Okami |Divinity = divine mirror |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small) 2 companies
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Takamusubi Daimyojin |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|November 17th|Q3028}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai)|1987}} ({{jalink|November 5th|Q2993}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}) |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Katsuragio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Katsurakio-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Katsurakio-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] '''Katsuraki Ojinja Shrine'' (Katsuraki Ojinja {{Sfn|Katsuraki Ojinja Shrine (Shikiuchi) | 1987}}/Kazuraki Ojinja {{Sfn|Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine (National History)}}) is {{jalink|Kochi Prefecture|Q134093}}{{jalink|Kochi City|Q200251}}{{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in Nunoshida. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}.In {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{jalink|Takami Musubi||Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{jalink|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{jalink|Katsuragi clan|Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{jalink|Early modern times|Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{jalink|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922}}, and that it is {{jalink|Takami Musubi||Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{jalink|Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{jalink|Katsuragi clan|Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{jalink|Isbun|Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{jalink|Tosa Shrine|Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{jalink|gunga|Q11643230|gunge}} of {{jalink|Tosa District|Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{jalink|Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{jalink|Japan Knowledge|Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{jalink|Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{jalink|Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{jalink|Miwa clan}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{jalink|Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{jalink|Hisao Hoga|Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{jalink|Kokubun River|Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{jalink|Tosa Shrine|Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}}5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} is {{jalink|Tosa Province|Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{jalink|Japanese name extract}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), in {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Katsuragi Ookami
Katsuragimaki Okami |Divinity = divine mirror |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small) 2 companies
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Takamusubi Daimyojin |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|November 17th|Q3028}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai)|1987}} ({{jalink|November 5th|Q2993}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}) |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Katsuragio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Katsurakio-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Katsurakio-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] '''Katsuraki Ojinja Shrine'' (Katsuraki Ojinja {{Sfn|Katsuraki Ojinja Shrine (Shikiuchi) | 1987}}/Kazuraki Ojinja {{Sfn|Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine (National History)}}) is {{jalink|Kochi Prefecture|Q134093}}{{jalink|Kochi City|Q200251}}{{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in Nunoshida. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}.In {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{jalink|Takami Musubi||Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{jalink|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{jalink|Katsuragi clan|Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{jalink|Early modern times|Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{jalink|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922}}, and that it is {{jalink|Takami Musubi||Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{jalink|Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{jalink|Katsuragi clan|Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{jalink|Isbun|Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{jalink|Tosa Shrine|Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{jalink|gunga|Q11643230|gunge}} of {{jalink|Tosa District|Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{jalink|Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{jalink|Japan Knowledge|Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{jalink|Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{jalink|Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{jalink|Miwa clan}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{jalink|Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{jalink|Hisao Hoga|Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{jalink|Kokubun River|Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{jalink|Tosa Shrine|Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}}5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} is {{jalink|Tosa Province|Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{jalink|Japanese name extract}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), in {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Takamusubi Daimyojin |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|November 17th|Q3028}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai)|1987}} ({{jalink|November 5th|Q2993}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}) |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Katsuragio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Katsurakio-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Katsurakio-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] '''Katsuraki Ojinja Shrine'' (Katsuraki Ojinja {{Sfn|Katsuraki Ojinja Shrine (Shikiuchi) | 1987}}/Kazuraki Ojinja {{Sfn|Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine (National History)}}) is {{jalink|Kochi Prefecture|Q134093}}{{jalink|Kochi City|Q200251}}{{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in Nunoshida. In {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}.In {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{jalink|Shintai|Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{jalink|Takami Musubi||Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{jalink|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{jalink|Katsuragi clan|Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Gose City|Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{jalink|Early modern times|Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{jalink|Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922}}, and that it is {{jalink|Takami Musubi||Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{jalink|Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{jalink|Katsuragi clan|Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{jalink|Isbun|Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{jalink|Tosa Shrine|Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{jalink|gunga|Q11643230|gunge}} of {{jalink|Tosa District|Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{jalink|Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{jalink|Japan Knowledge|Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{jalink|Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{jalink|Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{jalink|Miwa clan}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{jalink|Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{jalink|Hisao Hoga|Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{jalink|Kokubun River|Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{jalink|Tosa Shrine|Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{jalink|extension (Era name)|Q1327274|Extended}}5 years ({{jalink|927|Q30470}}) "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}} is {{jalink|Tosa Province|Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{jalink|Japanese name extract}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{jalink|Early modern period|Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} ({{jalink|1868|Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}), in {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}47 ({{jalink|1972|Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Takamusubi Daimyojin |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=November 17th|Q3028|WD=Q3028}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai)|1987}} ({{ill|lt=November 5th|Q2993|WD=Q2993}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}) |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Katsuragio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Katsurakio-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Katsurakio-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] '''Katsuraki Ojinja Shrine'' (Katsuraki Ojinja {{Sfn|Katsuraki Ojinja Shrine (Shikiuchi) | 1987}}/Kazuraki Ojinja {{Sfn|Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine (National History)}}) is {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093}}{{ill|lt=Kochi City|Q200251|WD=Q200251}}{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Nunoshida. In {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Takami Musubi||WD=|lt=Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}, and that it is {{ill|lt=Takami Musubi||WD=|lt=Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{ill|lt=Isbun|Q1585442|WD=Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230|lt=gunge}} of {{ill|lt=Tosa District|Q1132134|WD=Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{ill|lt=Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|WD=Q11507506|lt=New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{ill|lt=Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737|WD=Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{ill|lt=Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{ill||lt=Miwa clan|UNKNOWN}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{ill||lt=Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{ill|lt=Hisao Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{ill|lt=Kokubun River|Q11420533|WD=Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}}5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{ill||lt=Japanese name extract|UNKNOWN}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), in {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Original source === {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Precinct information board * Encyclopedia ** Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Takamusubi Daimyojin |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=November 17th|Q3028|WD=Q3028}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai)|1987}} ({{ill|lt=November 5th|Q2993|WD=Q2993}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}) |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Katsuragio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Katsurakio-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Katsurakio-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] '''Katsuraki Ojinja Shrine'' (Katsuraki Ojinja {{Sfn|Katsuraki Ojinja Shrine (Shikiuchi) | 1987}}/Kazuraki Ojinja {{Sfn|Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine (National History)}}) is {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093}}{{ill|lt=Kochi City|Q200251|WD=Q200251}}{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Nunoshida. In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Takami Musubi||WD=|lt=Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}, and that it is {{ill|lt=Takami Musubi||WD=|lt=Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{ill|lt=Isbun|Q1585442|WD=Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230|lt=gunge}} of {{ill|lt=Tosa District|Q1132134|WD=Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{ill|lt=Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|WD=Q11507506|lt=New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{ill|lt=Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737|WD=Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{ill|lt=Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|UNKNOWN}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{ill|lt=Hisao Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{ill|lt=Kokubun River|Q11420533|WD=Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}}5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name extract|UNKNOWN}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), in {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Original source === {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Precinct information board * Encyclopedia **
