Katsuragi Ookami
Katsuragimaki Okami |Divinity = divine mirror |Company rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small) 2 companies
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |Alternative name = Takamusubi Daimyojin |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=November 17th|Q3028|WD=Q3028}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai)|1987}} ({{ill|lt=November 5th|Q2993|WD=Q2993}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}) |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Katsuragio Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Katsurakio-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Katsurakio-jinja honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|main hall}}]] '''Katsuraki Ojinja Shrine'' (Katsuraki Ojinja {{Sfn|Katsuraki Ojinja Shrine (Shikiuchi) | 1987}}/Kazuraki Ojinja {{Sfn|Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine (National History)}}) is {{ill|lt=Kochi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093}}{{ill|lt=Kochi City|Q200251|WD=Q200251}}{{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in Nunoshida. In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Takami Musubi||WD=|lt=Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}, and that it is {{ill|lt=Takami Musubi||WD=|lt=Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{ill|lt=Isbun|Q1585442|WD=Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230|lt=gunge}} of {{ill|lt=Tosa District|Q1132134|WD=Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{ill|lt=Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|WD=Q11507506|lt=New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{ill|lt=Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737|WD=Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{ill|lt=Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{ill|lt=Hisao Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{ill|lt=Kokubun River|Q11420533|WD=Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}}5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name extract|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), in {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Original source === {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Precinct information board * Encyclopedia ** In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Takami Musubi|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}, and that it is {{ill|lt=Takami Musubi|UNKNOWN|WD=|lt=Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{ill|lt=Isbun|Q1585442|WD=Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230|lt=gunge}} of {{ill|lt=Tosa District|Q1132134|WD=Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{ill|lt=Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|WD=Q11507506|lt=New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{ill|lt=Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737|WD=Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{ill|lt=Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{ill|lt=Hisao Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{ill|lt=Kokubun River|Q11420533|WD=Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}}5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name extract|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), in {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Original source === {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Precinct information board * Encyclopedia ** In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Takami Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}, and that it is {{ill|lt=Takami Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{ill|lt=Isbun|Q1585442|WD=Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230|lt=gunge}} of {{ill|lt=Tosa District|Q1132134|WD=Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{ill|lt=Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|WD=Q11507506|lt=New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{ill|lt=Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737|WD=Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{ill|lt=Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{ill|lt=Hisao Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{ill|lt=Kokubun River|Q11420533|WD=Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}}5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name extract|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), in {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Original source === {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Precinct information board * Encyclopedia ** In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Takami Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}, and that it is {{ill|lt=Takami Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{ill|lt=Isbun|Q1585442|WD=Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230|lt=gunge}} of {{ill|lt=Tosa District|Q1132134|WD=Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{ill|lt=Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|WD=Q11507506|lt=New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{ill|lt=Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737|WD=Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{ill|lt=Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{ill|lt=Hisao Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{ill|lt=Kokubun River|Q11420533|WD=Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}}5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name extract|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), in {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Original source === {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Precinct information board * Encyclopedia ** In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Takami Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}, and that it is {{ill|lt=Takami Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{ill|lt=Isbun|Q1585442|WD=Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230|lt=gunge}} of {{ill|lt=Tosa District|Q1132134|WD=Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{ill|lt=Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|WD=Q11507506|lt=New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{ill|lt=Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737|WD=Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{ill|lt=Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{ill|lt=Hisao Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{ill|lt=Kokubun River|Q11420533|WD=Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}}5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name extract|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), in {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Original source === {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Precinct information board * Encyclopedia ** In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Takami Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}, and that it is {{ill|lt=Takami Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{ill|lt=Isbun|Q1585442|WD=Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230|lt=gunge}} of {{ill|lt=Tosa District|Q1132134|WD=Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{ill|lt=Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|WD=Q11507506|lt=New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{ill|lt=Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737|WD=Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{ill|lt=Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{ill|lt=Hisao Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{ill|lt=Kokubun River|Q11420533|WD=Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}}5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name extract|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), in {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Original source === {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Precinct information board * Encyclopedia ** In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}.In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), ``Katsuragi Shrine'' ( Katsurakihimejinja, formerly {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}), is enshrined here. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . {{ill|lt=Shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} is said to be a mirror {{Sfn|Meiji Shrine materials|1912}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Takami Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takami Musubi}}''' (Takami Musubi no Kami) * '''Katsurakio no Okami'' (Katsurakio no Okami) *: {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko no Mikoto}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. This Katsuragi Sotsuhiko is said to be the founder of the {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}} of the powerful clan in the Yamato-Katsuragi region (currently {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Gose City|Q847334|WD=Q847334}} area) {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. * '''Katsurakihime no Okami'' *: Katsuragi Sotsuhikohiko no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine (National History)}}. Became the enshrined deity of the jointly enshrined Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (National History)}}. === About the enshrined deity === In historical materials from {{ill|lt=Early modern times|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} onward, there are theories that the deity enshrined at Katsuragi Otsuhiko Shrine is {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922}}, and that it is {{ill|lt=Takami Musubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Takōsan Reimyo}}. Currently, the deity enshrined is based on both these theories. * Katsuragi Sotsuhiko Theory *: "{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Names and Names Roku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}" 'Newly Selected Names and Names' Roku, Izumi Province Emperor Betsu Fushishinjo. , this theory is based on the fact that Fushiomi (Fushiji) is a descendant of Katsuragi Sotsuhiko. It is said that the name of the place ``Fushida'' came about because this Fushi clan lived in the area and enshrined Katsuragi Sotsuhiko as their ancestral deity. * The theory of a spirit born from a high emperor*: "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" "Newly Selected Names and Names Roku" Kawachi Kunijinbetsu Tenjin Naojo Katsuragi. ``Newly Selected Names and Names Record'', Yamato Province, Jinbetsu, Tenjin, Katsuragi Death Note. A theory based on that Nao Katsuragi and Insun Katsuragi are the descendants of Takamitama no Mikoto (Takamitama no Mikoto/Takanosei no Mikoto). It is assumed that the shrine names that appear in the early modern period, such as ``Takamusubi Daimyo Jingu,'' ``Takatsuma Daimyo Jingu,'' and ``Takamusubisha,'' originate from this enshrined deity, and it is also assumed that the Katsuragi clan ({{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|WD=Q11620908}}) is the Hosai clan. However, there is also a theory that the {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}}, who are also descendants of spirits born to the Emperor, are the Hosai clan {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Furthermore, since the spirit born to the Emperor is a deity without sex, the ``Shikinai-sha Investigation Report'' considers the Katsuragi Sotsuhiko theory to be the most likely {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}} In addition to the above, there is also an Itsubun of the Tosa Province Fudoki {{ill|lt=Isbun|Q1585442|WD=Q1585442}}'Gajijikabutsu'. So,{{Quotation|There is a shrine inside the county house in Tsuchisa. The name of God is ``The Life of the Milky Way.'' If you go down the southern road, there is a shrine. The name of the god is ``Jyokawa Hime no Mikoto'', the daughter of the god of the Milky Way. The god of the Milky Way is Seri, the son of the great god of Tsuchisa. |Isbun (original text in Chinese) from ``Tosa no Kuni Fudoki'' quoted in ``Gagaji Questions and Answers'' The new text is based on ``New Edition of Japanese Classical Literature Complete Works 5 Fudoki'', Shogakukan, 2003 (Japan Knowledge Edition), p. 514. }}As such, there is a {{jal of Tsuchisa Okami ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}} enshrined deity) in {{ill|lt=gunga|Q11643230|WD=Q11643230|lt=gunge}} of {{ill|lt=Tosa District|Q1132134|WD=Q1132134}}. ink|Mikogami|Q352176}}There appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Amakawa no Mikoto'' (Amakawa no Mikoto), and there appears to be a shrine that enshrines ``Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto'' (Kiyokawa Hime no Mikoto), the daughter god of Tenga no Mikoto, on the road south of Gunge.. Tenga no Mikoto and Jokawa Hime no Mikoto are gods that are not mentioned in other documents, but as mentioned later, the Tosa County family is presumed to be near the former enshrinement site of Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, so there is a theory that they are compared to Katsuragi Otokogami and Katsuragi Maki no Kami History of Kochi Prefecture, Yamakawa Publishing, 2001, pp. 47-48. "{{ill|lt=Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature|Q11507506|WD=Q11507506|lt=New Edition of Complete Works of Japanese Classical Literature}} 5 Fudoki" Shogakukan, 2003 ({{ill|lt=Japan Knowledge|Q11309996|WD=Q11309996}} edition), pp. 513-514. . The Tosa County family is thought to have been established by incorporating the shrine of Tenga no Mikoto, which had existed for a long time., and it is presumed that this area was the center of {{ill|lt=Tosa Kunizo|Q21653737|WD=Q21653737}} (Tosa Kunizo), a powerful clan, before the establishment of Tosa County. This Tosa Kunizo is said to be descended from the {{ill|lt=Kamo Asaomi clan|Q122979457|WD=Q122979457}}, which descends from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Miwa clan|1=UNKNOWN}} in the Yamato-Katsuragi region.It is also related to our company's name, "Katsuragi." In that case, it is very likely that the original enshrined deity was '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mishio Takahikone no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the ancestor of Tsusa Kunizo, who is enshrined in Takagamo in Katsuragi. {{ill|lt=Hisao Hoga|Q11452691|WD=Q11452691}}' Old shrines and priests in central Tosa], Koju Ki no Bosama, 1998. . However, even though Katsuragi Ogami was the son of Tsuchisa Okami in ancient times, there is no known ritual relationship between the current Tosa Shrine and Katsuragi Otoko Shrine. === History === [[File:Katsurakihime-jinja (old place).JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Katsurakihime-jinja old place}}]] The date of establishment of both Katsuragi Otoshi Shrine and Katsuragi Maki Shrine is unknown. According to legend, both shrines were originally enshrined in the same precincts to the southeast of the current shrine area (currently south of the village of Shimotsuke), but Katsuragi Shrine was moved to the southeast ({{Coord|33|34|55.01|N|133|36|27.87|E|region:JP-39_type:landm ark|name=Old location of Katsuragi Shrine}}), Katsuragi Otoko Shrine also moved to its current location in the northwest to avoid the rising waters of the {{ill|lt=Kokubun River|Q11420533|WD=Q11420533}}. In addition, it is an itbun of "Tosa Province Fudoki" "Shaku Nihongi" volume 12 (Shogi 8) Hitotsubushijinjo or volume 15 (Shogi 11) Tosa no Okami Ichito Shinyu Emperor's article is included in the "Tosa Province Fudoki" Isbun. According to , the Tosa County family was located 4 ri (approximately 2 kilometers) east of Tsuchisa Takagamo Taisha Shrine ({{ill|lt=Tosa Shrine|Q704890|WD=Q704890}}), but it is assumed that the location was near the former enshrinement area, {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}.{{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=Extended}}5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmeicho}} is {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330}}{{j alink|Tosa County|Q1132134}}, it is listed as a pair with "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine", and is also known as "Katsuragi Otoko Shrine" and "Katsuragimaki Shrine". alink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} {{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. Their pronunciations are ``Katsurakiwono'' and ``Katsurakihimeno,'' respectively {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Also, among the place names that appear in the ``{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Japanese name extract|1=UNKNOWN}}'', the current enshrined place is presumed to be near the eastern part of Tosa-go, Tosa District. Its subsequent history is unknown, and it is thought to have fallen into disrepair as there is no mention in the Chosokabe District Public Prosecutors Office. According to "Minamijishi", in the {{ill|lt=Early modern period|Q5308718|WD=Q5308718}} Katsuragi Ojinja Shrine had 18 generations of shrines, 1 and 22 generations of Taji, and Nishiyama-dera (located in the south) was {{ja link|Bettoji Temple|Q11396755}}, and it is said that there were 34 generations of shrines and 10 generations of Taji at Katsuragi Shrine {{Sfn|Katsuragio Shrine (Kadokawa)|1986}}.In the early modern period, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was called ``Takamusubi Daimyo Shrine,'' but in the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} ({{ill|lt=1868|Q7717|WD=Q7717}}) it was renamed to its current name {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In the 5th year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}), in {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}, Katsuragi Otoko Shrine was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316} In }}, Katsuragi Shrine was listed as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}{{Sfn|Katsuragi Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}47 ({{ill|lt=1972|Q2476|WD=Q2476}}), Katsuragi Shrine was enshrined with Katsuragi Otoko Shrine, which remains today {{Sfn|Katsuragi Otoko Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Original source === {{Reflist|group="Original"}} === Source === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Precinct information board * Encyclopedia **
